unauthenticated_state_change.description: A state-changing API operation is documented without an authentication requirement.
unauthenticated_state_change.impact: Attackers may be able to modify data or trigger workflow actions if runtime controls match the documentation gap.
unauthenticated_state_change.remediation: Require explicit authentication for the operation, document the expected security scheme in OpenAPI/Postman, and verify the runtime gateway or service rejects unauthenticated requests.

sensitive_unauthenticated.description: A sensitive endpoint path is documented without authentication.
sensitive_unauthenticated.impact: Sensitive account, payment, administrative, token, or internal resources may be exposed if implementation follows the imported definition.
sensitive_unauthenticated.remediation: Document and enforce authentication, add authorization requirements for privileged resources, and review route ownership with the API team.

deprecated_endpoint.description: A deprecated API operation remains present in the imported definition.
deprecated_endpoint.impact: Older endpoints are often less monitored and may retain weaker controls or obsolete data contracts.
deprecated_endpoint.remediation: Confirm whether the endpoint is still deployed, publish a retirement date, block new consumers, and remove the route when migration is complete.

missing_security_scheme.description: The imported API definition does not document a reusable security scheme.
missing_security_scheme.impact: Consumers and reviewers cannot consistently understand expected authentication posture.
missing_security_scheme.remediation: Define OpenAPI securitySchemes or Postman auth configuration, apply global defaults where appropriate, and explicitly override only public endpoints.

inconsistent_auth.description: Authentication requirements vary across related endpoint inventory.
inconsistent_auth.impact: Inconsistent documentation can hide accidental public routes or weaken review coverage.
inconsistent_auth.remediation: Normalize security requirements by resource family, document public exceptions, and add CI checks for unexpected unauthenticated operations.

sensitive_response_field.description: A response schema or example documents a sensitive field name.
sensitive_response_field.impact: Sensitive values may be exposed to clients or logs if response filtering is not enforced.
sensitive_response_field.remediation: Remove sensitive fields from response DTOs, apply field-level authorization, redact secrets before serialization, and add contract tests for response payloads.

missing_rate_limit.description: The imported definition does not document rate-limit or quota metadata.
missing_rate_limit.impact: Clients and reviewers cannot determine intended resource-consumption controls.
missing_rate_limit.remediation: Document rate-limit headers, quota behavior, retry guidance, and per-consumer throttling expectations.

missing_operation_id.description: An operation lacks an operationId and summary.
missing_operation_id.impact: Undocumented operations are harder to inventory, test, monitor, and assign ownership.
missing_operation_id.remediation: Add stable operation IDs, summaries, owner tags, and change-control metadata to the API contract.

insecure_http_server.description: A server URL uses cleartext HTTP.
insecure_http_server.impact: Clients may transmit tokens or sensitive data without transport protection.
insecure_http_server.remediation: Use HTTPS server URLs only, redirect HTTP to HTTPS, and verify TLS configuration at the gateway.

jwt_risk.description: Decoded JWT metadata contains security risk indicators.
jwt_risk.impact: Weak token metadata can increase authentication and session-management risk if used in production.
jwt_risk.remediation: Use strong signing algorithms, short lifetimes, issuer and audience validation, minimal claims, and do not place secrets in tokens.
